heading: Wetland reserve easements
label: 3865c Wetland reserve easements

children.text: wetland reserve easements and related wetland reserve easement plans; and
technical assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: 30-year easements;

children.label: B
children.text: permanent easements;

children.label: C
children.text: easements for the maximum duration allowed under applicable State laws; or

children.label: D
children.text: as an option for Indian tribes only, 30-year contracts.

children.label: i
children.text: land established to trees under the conservation reserve program, except in cases where the Secretary determines it would further the purposes of this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: farmed wetlands or converted wetlands where the conversion was not commenced prior to .December 23, 1985

children.label: i
children.text: the new ownership was acquired by will or succession as a result of the death of the previous owner;

children.label: I
children.text: the ownership change occurred because of foreclosure on the land; and

children.label: II
children.text: immediately before the foreclosure, the owner of the land exercises a right of redemption from the mortgage holder in accordance with State law; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary determines that the land was acquired under circumstances that give adequate assurances that such land was not acquired for the purposes of placing it in the program.

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: A Criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall establish evaluation and ranking criteria for offers from landowners under this section to maximize the benefit of Federal investment under the program.

children.label: i
children.text: the conservation benefits of obtaining a wetland reserve easement, including the potential environmental benefits if the land was removed from agricultural production;

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost effectiveness of each wetland reserve easement, so as to maximize the environmental benefits per dollar expended;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether the landowner or another person is offering to contribute financially to the cost of the wetland reserve easement to leverage Federal funds; and

children.label: iv
children.text: such other factors as the Secretary determines are necessary to carry out the purposes of the program.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: C Priority
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority to acquiring wetland reserve easements based on the value of the wetland reserve easement for protecting and enhancing habitat for migratory birds and other wildlife or improving water quality.

children.label: A
children.text: grant an easement on such land to the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: authorize the implementation of a wetland reserve easement plan developed for the eligible land under subsection (f);

children.label: C
children.text: create and record an appropriate deed restriction in accordance with applicable State law to reflect the easement agreed to;

children.label: D
children.text: provide a written statement of consent to such easement signed by those holding a security interest in the land;

children.label: E
children.text: comply with the terms and conditions of the easement and any related agreements; and

children.label: F
children.text: permanently retire any existing base history for the land on which the easement has been obtained.

children.label: I
children.text: repairs, improvements, and inspections on the land that are necessary to maintain existing public drainage systems; and

children.label: II
children.text: owners to control public access on the easement areas while identifying access routes to be used for restoration activities and management and easement monitoring;

children.label: I
children.text: the alteration of wildlife habitat and other natural features of such land, unless specifically authorized by the Secretary;

children.label: aa
children.text: to comply with Federal or State noxious weed control laws;

children.label: bb
children.text: to comply with a Federal or State emergency pest treatment program; or

children.label: cc
children.text: to meet habitat needs of specific wildlife species;

children.label: III
children.text: any activities to be carried out on the owner’s or successor’s land that is immediately adjacent to, and functionally related to, the land that is subject to the easement if such activities will alter, degrade, or otherwise diminish the functional value of the eligible land; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the adoption of any other practice that would tend to defeat the purposes of the program, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: provide for the efficient and effective establishment of wetland functions and values; and

children.label: iv
children.text: include such additional provisions as the Secretary determines are desirable to carry out the program or facilitate the practical administration thereof.

children.heading: Violation
children.label: B Violation
children.text: On the violation of a term or condition of a wetland reserve easement, the wetland reserve easement shall remain in force and the Secretary may require the owner to refund all or part of any payments received by the owner under the program, with interest on the payments as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Land subject to a wetland reserve easement may be used for compatible economic uses, including such activities as hunting and fishing, managed timber harvest, water management, or periodic haying or grazing, if such use is specifically permitted by the wetland reserve easement plan developed for the land under subsection (f) and is consistent with the long-term protection and enhancement of the wetland resources for which the easement was established.

children.label: I
children.text: request and consider the advice of the applicable State technical committee established under about the 1 or more types of uses that may be authorized to be conducted on land subject to a wetland reserve easement, including the frequency, timing, and intensity of those uses;section 3861(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: consider the ability of an authorized use to facilitate the practical administration and management of that land; and

children.label: III
children.text: ensure that an authorized use furthers the functions and values for which the wetland reserve easement was established.

children.label: I
children.text: is compatible with the land subject to the easement;

children.label: II
children.text: is consistent with the historical natural uses of the land and the long-term protection and enhancement goals for which the easement was established; and

children.label: III
children.text: complies with the wetland reserve easement plan developed for the land under subsection (f) or a grazing management plan that is consistent with the wetland reserve easement plan and has been reviewed, and modified as necessary, at least every 5 years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the agreement provides for a commensurate reduction in the easement payment to account for the grazing value, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: the fair market value of the land, as determined by the Secretary, using the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice or an areawide market analysis or survey;

children.label: II
children.text: the amount corresponding to a geographical cap, as determined by the Secretary in regulations; or

children.label: III
children.text: the offer made by the landowner.

children.heading: Other
children.label: ii Other
children.text: Compensation for a 30-year contract or 30-year wetland reserve easement shall be not less than 50 percent, but not more than 75 percent, of the compensation that would be paid for a permanent wetland reserve easement.

children.heading: Form of payment
children.label: B Form of payment
children.text: Compensation for a wetland reserve easement shall be provided by the Secretary in the form of a cash payment, in an amount determined under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Easements valued at $500,000 or less
children.label: i Easements valued at $500,000 or less
children.text: For wetland reserve easements valued at $500,000 or less, the Secretary may provide payments in not more than 10 annual payments.

children.heading: Easements valued at more than $500,000
children.label: ii Easements valued at more than $500,000
children.text: For wetland reserve easements valued at more than $500,000, the Secretary may provide payments in at least 5, but not more than 10 annual payments, except that, if the Secretary determines it would further the purposes of the program, the Secretary may make a lump-sum payment for such an easement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide financial assistance to owners of eligible land to carry out the establishment of conservation measures and practices and protect wetland functions and values, including necessary maintenance activities, as set forth in a wetland reserve easement plan developed for the eligible land under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a permanent wetland reserve easement, pay an amount that is not less than 75 percent, but not more than 100 percent, of the eligible costs, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a 30-year contract or 30-year wetland reserve easement, pay an amount that is not less than 50 percent, but not more than 75 percent, of the eligible costs, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall assist owners in complying with the terms and conditions of a wetland reserve easement.

children.heading: Contracts or agreements
children.label: 2 Contracts or agreements
children.text: The Secretary may enter into 1 or more contracts with private entities or agreements with a State, nongovernmental organization, or Indian tribe to carry out necessary restoration, enhancement, or maintenance of a wetland reserve easement if the Secretary determines that the contract or agreement will advance the purposes of the program.

children.heading: Wetland reserve enhancement option
children.label: e Wetland reserve enhancement option
children.text: The Secretary may enter into 1 or more agreements with a State (including a political subdivision or agency of a State), nongovernmental organization, or Indian tribe to carry out a special wetland reserve enhancement option that the Secretary determines would advance the purposes of program.

children.label: i
children.text: for any eligible land subject to a wetland reserve easement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that restores, protects, enhances, manages, maintains, and monitors the eligible land subject to the wetland reserve easements acquired under this section.

children.heading: Practices and activities
children.label: B Practices and activities
children.text: A wetland reserve easement plan under subparagraph (A) shall include practices and activities, including repair or replacement, that are necessary to restore and maintain the enrolled land and the functions and values of the wetland subject to a wetland reserve easement.

children.label: A
children.text: substantially support or benefit migratory waterfowl or other wetland wildlife; or

children.label: B
children.text: meet local resource concerns or needs (including as an element of a regional, State, or local wildlife initiative or plan).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may delegate any of the management, monitoring, and enforcement responsibilities of the Secretary under this section to other Federal or State agencies that have the appropriate authority, expertise, and resources necessary to carry out such delegated responsibilities, or to conservation organizations if the Secretary determines the organization has similar expertise and resources.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not delegate any of the monitoring or enforcement responsibilities under this section to conservation organizations.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to any easement restoration obligation under subsection (c), as soon as possible after the obligation is incurred; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to any annual easement payment obligation incurred by the Secretary, as soon as possible after October 1 of each calendar year.

children.heading: Payments to others
children.label: B Payments to others
children.text: If an owner who is entitled to a payment under this section dies, becomes incompetent, is otherwise unable to receive such payment, or is succeeded by another person or entity who renders or completes the required performance, the Secretary shall make such payment, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary and without regard to any other provision of law, in such manner as the Secretary determines is fair and reasonable in light of all of the circumstances.

children.heading: Application
children.label: g Application
children.text: The relevant provisions of this section shall also apply to a 30-year contract.
